heading: Confidentiality of abused person’s address
label: 12321 Confidentiality of abused person’s address

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: a Regulations
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , the United States Postal Service shall promulgate regulations to secure the confidentiality of domestic violence shelters and abused persons’ addresses.September 13, 1994
in the case of an individual, the presentation to an appropriate postal official of a valid, outstanding protection order; and

in the case of a domestic violence shelter, the presentation to an appropriate postal authority of proof from a State domestic violence coalition that meets the requirements of section 10410 of title 42 verifying that the organization is a domestic violence shelter.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Disclosure for certain purposes
children.label: c Disclosure for certain purposes
children.text: The regulations under subsection (a) shall not prohibit the disclosure of addresses to State or Federal agencies for legitimate law enforcement or other governmental purposes.

children.heading: Existing compilations
children.label: d Existing compilations
children.text: Compilations of addresses existing at the time at which order is presented to an appropriate postal official shall be excluded from the scope of the regulations under subsection (a).
